---
title: "STA137 Final Project"
author: "Caspar Leung and Elizabeth Louie"
date: "2026-03-05"
output: html_document
---


```{r}
library(forecast)
library(ggplot2)

# Data Acquisition 

# import data file
df <- read.csv("multiTimeline.csv", skip = 2, header = TRUE)

# rename columns
colnames(df) <- c("Time", "Interest")

df$Time <- substr(df$Time, 1, 19)

# check the first few rows
head(df)
summary(df$Interest)
```

```{r}
# Data Visualization 

# scatter plot of original series 
ggplot(df, aes(x = Time, y = Interest)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  labs(
    title = "Scatter Plot: Search Interest Over Time",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Interest"
  )

# histogram showing the distribution of Interest rating
hist(df$Interest,
     main = "Histogram of BTS Search Interest",
     xlab = "Interest")

```

```{r}
# Data Preparation 

# identify outliers
df[which.max(df$Interest), ]

ggplot(df, aes(x = Time, y = Interest)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_point(
    data = df[df$Time == "2026-01-01", ],
    color = "red",
    size = 3
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  labs(
    title = "Scatter Plot: Search Interest Over Time",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Interest Score"
  )

# removal of outliers 
df_clean <- df[df$Time != "2026-01-01", ]

# scatter plot after outlier removal 
ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = Time, y = Interest)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Scatter Plot: Search Interest Over Time (Outlier Removed)",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Interest Score"
  )

# check for missing values 
sum(is.na(df$Interest)) # output = 0 

```


```{r}
# Data Preparation 

# convert data to time series object   
bts_ts <- ts(df_clean$Interest, frequency = 12, start = c(2021,3)) 

# boxcox lambda approximation for varaince stability 
BoxCox.lambda(bts_ts) # output -0.4964918

# apply boxcox transformation 
bts_bc <- BoxCox(bts_ts, lambda = -0.4964918)

# plot of transformed series 
plot(bts_bc,
     main = "Histogram of Box-Cox Transformed BTS Interest",
     ylab = "Transformed Interest",
     xlab = "Time")

# histogram 
hist(bts_bc,
     main = "Histogram of Log-Transformed BTS Interest",
     xlab = "log(Interest)")

# QQ plot
qqnorm(bts_bc)
qqline(bts_bc)
```




```{r}
# Analyzing the "Smooth" Component: Trend and Seasonality Elimination 

# early period 
bts_early <- window(bts_bc, end = c(2022, 6))

# later period 
bts_late <- window(bts_bc, start = c(2022, 6))
# time series object of original data with outlier 
bts_ts_full <- ts(df$Interest, frequency = 12, start = c(2021,3))
BoxCox_full <- BoxCox.lambda(bts_ts_full)
bts_bc_full <- BoxCox(bts_ts_full, lambda = BoxCox_full)

weights <- c(0.5, rep(1, 11), 0.5)/12
hatm_early <- stats::filter(bts_early, filter = weights, sides = 2)
hatm_late  <- stats::filter(bts_late,  filter = weights, sides = 2)
hatm_full <- filter(bts_bc_full, sides = 2, c(0.5, rep(1,11), 0.5)/12)



decomp <- decompose(bts_bc, type = "additive")

# extract components for your analysis
trend_component_fore    <- decomp$trend    # This is mt
seasonal_component_fore  <- decomp$seasonal # This is st
rough_component_fore     <- decomp$random   # This is Yt (the residuals)

# estimated trends without outlier 
plot(bts_bc,
     main="Trend Estimate Not including outl",
     ylab="Interest",
     xlab="Time")
lines(hatm_early, col="blue", lwd=2)
lines(hatm_late, col="red", lwd=2)

# estimated trend plot with outlier 
plot(bts_bc_full,
     main="Trend Estimate Including Outlier",
     ylab="Interest",
     xlab="Time")
lines(hatm_full, col="purple", lwd=2)

# visualize the whole process
plot(decomp)

# check if the Residuals are White Noise
# ACF and Histogram
checkresiduals(decomp$random)

# residual after trend & seasonality remove
plot(decomp$random, 
     main = "Residuals After Trend & Seasonality Removal",
     ylab = "Residual Value",
     xlab = "Year",
     col = "blue")

abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)


```








```{r}
#Analyzing the “rough” component: model fitting & diagnostics


# Residual analysis 
checkresiduals(decomp$random)

smooth_resid <- na.omit(decomp$random)
#ordered

# Fit candidate models 
fit_ar1 <- Arima(smooth_resid, order = c(1,0,0))
fit_ma1 <- Arima(smooth_resid, order = c(0,0,1))
fit_arma11 <- Arima(smooth_resid, order = c(1,0,1))

# Compare by AIC
AIC(fit_ar1, fit_ma1, fit_arma11)

# Choose best AR(1)
best_fit <- fit_ar1 # model with lowest AIC 

# Diagnostics on innovations
innov <- residuals(best_fit)

acf(na.omit(innov), main = "ACF of ARMA Innovations")
pacf(na.omit(innov), main = "PACF of ARMA Innovations")

#conclusion either AR(1) or ARMA(1,1)
```

```{r}
#Predict future values:
h <- 12  
# series WITHOUT the outlier
n <- length(bts_bc) 
weights <- c(0.5, rep(1, 11), 0.5)/12

# trend calculated on clean data) ---
hatm_clean <- stats::filter(bts_bc, filter = weights, sides = 2)
last_trend <- as.numeric(na.omit(hatm_clean))
last_trend <- last_trend[length(last_trend)] 
trend_forecast <- rep(last_trend, h) 

# seasonality
decomp_clean <- decompose(bts_bc, type = "additive")
# Extract the 12-month pattern starting from January to align with forecast start
seasonal_cycle <- as.numeric(decomp_clean$seasonal)[1:12]
seasonal_forecast <- rep(seasonal_cycle, length.out = h)

# rough component ar(1)
rough_clean <- na.omit(decomp_clean$random)
fit_ar1_clean <- Arima(rough_clean, order = c(1, 0, 0))

rough_pred_obj <- predict(fit_ar1_clean, n.ahead = h)
rough_forecast <- as.numeric(rough_pred_obj$pred)
rough_se <- as.numeric(rough_pred_obj$se) 

# combine + transform
final_prediction_bc <- trend_forecast + seasonal_forecast + rough_forecast

# Using your stable lambda value
lambda_val <- -0.4964918 
inv_bc <- function(x, lam) { (x * lam + 1)^(1/lam) }

final_prediction <- inv_bc(final_prediction_bc, lambda_val)
lower_pi <- inv_bc(final_prediction_bc - 1.96 * rough_se, lambda_val)
upper_pi <- inv_bc(final_prediction_bc + 1.96 * rough_se, lambda_val)

# --- 6. VISUALIZATION PREP ---
# Define the start for march 2026
forecast_start_date <- c(2026, 3)

forecast_ts <- ts(final_prediction, start = forecast_start_date, frequency = 12)
lower_ts <- ts(lower_pi, start = forecast_start_date, frequency = 12)
upper_ts <- ts(upper_pi, start = forecast_start_date, frequency = 12)

# Calculate y_max here so the plot() function doesn't error out
y_max <- max(c(as.numeric(bts_ts), as.numeric(upper_pi)), na.rm = TRUE)

# --- 7. THE FINAL PLOT ---
plot(bts_ts, 
     xlim = c(2021, 2027.5), 
     ylim = c(0, y_max + 10),
     main = "BTS Forecast: 2026 Projections (Excluding Outlier)",
     ylab = "Interest Score", 
     xlab = "Year",
     lwd = 2)

lines(forecast_ts, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
lines(lower_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)
lines(upper_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)

legend("topright", 
       legend = c("Observed Data", "Point Forecast", "95% PI"),
       col = c("black", "blue", "red"), 
       lty = c(1, 1, 2), 
       lwd = c(2, 2, 1),
       cex = 0.8)
```

```{r forecasting with outlier}
# Create the full time series object (including the outlier)
bts_ts_full <- ts(df$Interest, frequency = 12, start = c(2021, 3))

# variance stabilization
lambda_full <- BoxCox.lambda(bts_ts_full) 
# Apply BoxCox
bts_bc_full <- BoxCox(bts_ts_full, lambda = lambda_full)

# check for symmetry
#plot(bts_bc_full, main = "Box-Cox Transformed BTS Interest (Full Data)")

# Trend estimation using moving average
hatm_full <- filter(bts_bc_full, sides = 2, c(0.5, rep(1, 11), 0.5)/12)

# seasonal estimation
# remove trend
detrended_full <- bts_bc_full - hatm_full

# calculate monthly seasonal effects
A_full <- matrix(detrended_full, ncol = 12, byrow = TRUE)
mu_full <- colMeans(A_full, na.rm = TRUE)

# check seasonal components sum to 0
hats_full <- mu_full - mean(mu_full)
seasonal_full_ts <- ts(rep(hats_full, length.out = length(bts_ts_full)), 
                       start = c(2021, 3), frequency = 12)

# "Rough" Component: extract residuals 
rough_resid_full <- bts_bc_full - hatm_full - seasonal_full_ts
rough_resid_full <- na.omit(rough_resid_full) # Remove NAs from filter

# residual diagnostics
#acf(as.numeric(rough_resid_full), main = "ACF of Residuals (Full Data)")
#pacf(as.numeric(rough_resid_full), main = "PACF of Residuals (Full Data)")

# fit the ARMA Model
# Compare AIC to find the best fit 
fit_full <- Arima(rough_resid_full, order = c(1, 0, 0)) #

# Forecast 12 Months Ahead
h_full <- 12

# Predict Trend linear/quadratic continuation method
t_vals <- 1:length(bts_bc_full)
trend_model <- lm(bts_bc_full ~ t_vals + I(t_vals^2))
future_t_full <- (length(bts_bc_full) + 1):(length(bts_bc_full) + h_full)
trend_pred <- predict(trend_model, newdata = data.frame(t_vals = future_t_full))

# Predict Seasonality
seasonal_pred <- rep(hats_full, length.out = h_full)

# Predict Rough Component
rough_pred_obj <- predict(fit_full, n.ahead = h_full)
rough_pred <- rough_pred_obj$pred
rough_se <- rough_pred_obj$se

# Combine all components
final_forecast_bc <- trend_pred + seasonal_pred + rough_pred

# Step 8: Inverse Box-Cox to return to original scale
final_forecast <- InvBoxCox(final_forecast_bc, lambda = lambda_full)

# Plotting the results

plot(bts_ts_full, xlim = c(2021, 2027.5), main = "BTS Search Interest Forecast (With Outlier)")
lines(ts(final_forecast, start = c(2026, 3), frequency = 12), col = "red", lwd = 2)
```

```{r forecasting up to outlier}

# truncate the data at the outlier index = 59
raw_series_outlier <- ts(df$Interest[1:59], frequency = 12, start = c(2021, 3))


# stabilize variance
power_transform_val <- BoxCox.lambda(raw_series_outlier)
stabilized_series <- BoxCox(raw_series_outlier, lambda = power_transform_val)


# estimate trend using a moving average filter
moving_avg_trend <- filter(stabilized_series, sides = 2, filter = c(0.5, rep(1, 11), 0.5)/12)

# estimate seasonality
centered_series <- stabilized_series - moving_avg_trend
# average the months
seasonal_matrix <- matrix(centered_series[7:54], ncol = 12, byrow = TRUE) 
monthly_effects <- colMeans(seasonal_matrix, na.rm = TRUE)
final_seasonal_pattern <- monthly_effects - mean(monthly_effects)

seasonal_overlay <- ts(rep(final_seasonal_pattern, length.out = 59), 
                       start = c(2021, 3), frequency = 12)

# --- 4. RESIDUAL ANALYSIS (ROUGH COMPONENT) ---
stochastic_resid <- stabilized_series - moving_avg_trend - seasonal_overlay
stochastic_resid <- na.omit(stochastic_resid)

# ACF and PACF
acf(as.numeric(stochastic_resid), main = "ACF: Residuals Ending at Outlier")
pacf(as.numeric(stochastic_resid), main = "PACF: Residuals Ending at Outlier")

# fit AR(1) to the rough component
ar1_residual_model <- arima(stochastic_resid, order = c(1, 0, 0))
stochastic_forecast_obj <- predict(ar1_residual_model, n.ahead = 12)

# predict trend forward using quadratic regression
time_indices <- 1:59
quadratic_trend_fit <- lm(stabilized_series ~ time_indices + I(time_indices^2))
future_indices <- 60:71
predicted_trend <- predict(quadratic_trend_fit, newdata = data.frame(time_indices = future_indices))

# Predict Seasonality forward by repeating the cycle
predicted_season <- rep(final_seasonal_pattern, length.out = 12)

# Combine predicted parts and reverse Box-Cox to original scale
total_forecast_bc <- predicted_trend + predicted_season + stochastic_forecast_obj$pred
final_interest_forecast <- InvBoxCox(total_forecast_bc, lambda = power_transform_val)

y_upper_limit <- max(c(raw_series_outlier, final_interest_forecast), na.rm = TRUE)

plot(raw_series_outlier, 
     xlim = c(2021, 2027.5), 
     ylim = c(0, y_upper_limit + 15),
     main = "BTS Search Interest: Forecast from Outlier Point",
     ylab = "Interest Score",
     xlab = "Year",
     lwd = 2)

# Convert forecast to TS object starting Feb 2026
forecast_start_date <- c(2026, 2)
lines(ts(final_interest_forecast, start = forecast_start_date, frequency = 12), 
      col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2)

# Add Legend
legend("topleft", 
       legend = c("Observed Data (Ending at Outlier)", "Forecasted Interest"),
       col = c("black", "darkgreen"), 
       lty = 1, lwd = 2, cex = 0.8)

cat("Forecasted point for Feb 2026 (immediately after outlier):", final_interest_forecast[1])

```
